content.text: Multimodal UX extends the text chat paradigm to accept images, PDFs, URLs, and voice as inputs, and to render images, structured data, and audio as outputs. Claude natively understands images alongside text, so the primary engineering challenge is safely collecting, encoding, and routing non-text inputs to the API — not prompting. This section covers drag-and-drop image upload, PDF-to-image conversion, webpage screenshots, voice transcription, and the React components that tie them together in a single unified input.

attrs.alt: Multimodal UX: five input channels (text, image, PDF, URL, audio) feeding Claude, and five output channels

content.text: Claude accepts images as base64-encoded content blocks alongside text. Support drag-and-drop, click-to-browse, and paste (Ctrl+V) to cover all user workflows. Validate MIME type and file size on the server before encoding.

from fastapi import FastAPI, UploadFile, File, Form
from anthropic import AsyncAnthropic
import base64, imghdr

app = FastAPI()
client = AsyncAnthropic()

ALLOWED_MIME = {"image/jpeg", "image/png", "image/gif", "image/webp"}
MAX_BYTES = 5 * 1024 * 1024  # 5 MB

@app.post("/chat/{session_id}/message-with-image")
async def message_with_image(
    session_id: str,
    text: str = Form(...),
    image: UploadFile = File(None),
):
    history = conversations.get(session_id, [])
    content: list[dict] = []

    if image:
        raw = await image.read()
        if len(raw) > MAX_BYTES:
            return {"error": "Image exceeds 5 MB limit"}, 400
        if image.content_type not in ALLOWED_MIME:
            return {"error": "Unsupported image type"}, 400
        b64 = base64.standard_b64encode(raw).decode()
        content.append({
            "type": "image",
            "source": {"type": "base64", "media_type": image.content_type, "data": b64},
        })

    if text:
        content.append({"type": "text", "text": text})

    history.append({"role": "user", "content": content})

    response = await client.messages.create(
        model="claude-opus-4-8",
        max_tokens=2048,
        messages=history,
    )
    reply = response.content[0].text
    history.append({"role": "assistant", "content": reply})
    conversations[session_id] = history
    return {"reply": reply}

Claude has no native PDF parser — convert each page to a PNG image and send them as a sequence of image blocks. For text-heavy PDFs where layout does not matter, fall back to pypdf text extraction which is cheaper and faster.

import io
from pypdf import PdfReader          # pip install pypdf
from PIL import Image                 # pip install pillow
import base64

def pdf_to_image_blocks(pdf_bytes: bytes, max_pages: int = 10) -> list[dict]:
    """Convert PDF pages to base64 PNG image blocks for Claude."""
    try:
        # Try rendering with pdf2image for fidelity (requires poppler)
        from pdf2image import convert_from_bytes
        images = convert_from_bytes(pdf_bytes, dpi=144, first_page=1, last_page=max_pages)
    except ImportError:
        # Fallback: extract raw text via pypdf
        reader = PdfReader(io.BytesIO(pdf_bytes))
        text = "\n\n".join(
            page.extract_text() or "" for page in reader.pages[:max_pages]
        )
        return [{"type": "text", "text": f"[PDF text]\n{text}"}]

    blocks = []
    for img in images[:max_pages]:
        buf = io.BytesIO()
        img.save(buf, format="PNG", optimize=True)
        b64 = base64.standard_b64encode(buf.getvalue()).decode()
        blocks.append({
            "type": "image",
            "source": {"type": "base64", "media_type": "image/png", "data": b64},
        })
    return blocks

@app.post("/chat/{session_id}/upload-pdf")
async def upload_pdf(session_id: str, file: UploadFile, question: str = Form("")):
    raw = await file.read()
    image_blocks = pdf_to_image_blocks(raw, max_pages=8)
    if question:
        image_blocks.append({"type": "text", "text": question})
    history = conversations.setdefault(session_id, [])
    history.append({"role": "user", "content": image_blocks})
    response = await client.messages.create(
        model="claude-opus-4-8", max_tokens=4096, messages=history
    )
    reply = response.content[0].text
    history.append({"role": "assistant", "content": reply})
    return {"reply": reply, "pages_processed": len(image_blocks) - (1 if question else 0)}
Warn users when a PDF exceeds the page limit. For very long documents, summarise sections iteratively: process 8 pages at a time, accumulate summaries, then synthesise.

Agents that research URLs benefit from seeing the page visually rather than relying on raw HTML. Playwright captures a high-fidelity screenshot that Claude can describe, extract data from, or compare against a reference.

from playwright.async_api import async_playwright
import base64

async def screenshot_url(url: str, width: int = 1280, height: int = 800) -> str:
    """Capture a webpage screenshot and return as base64 PNG."""
    async with async_playwright() as pw:
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch()
        page = await browser.new_page(viewport={"width": width, "height": height})
        await page.goto(url, wait_until="networkidle", timeout=15_000)
        png = await page.screenshot(full_page=False)
        await browser.close()
    return base64.standard_b64encode(png).decode()

@app.post("/chat/{session_id}/analyse-url")
async def analyse_url(session_id: str, body: dict):
    url = body["url"]
    question = body.get("question", "Describe this webpage.")
    b64 = await screenshot_url(url)
    content = [
        {"type": "image", "source": {"type": "base64", "media_type": "image/png", "data": b64}},
        {"type": "text", "text": question},
    ]
    history = conversations.setdefault(session_id, [])
    history.append({"role": "user", "content": content})
    response = await client.messages.create(
        model="claude-opus-4-8", max_tokens=2048, messages=history
    )
    reply = response.content[0].text
    history.append({"role": "assistant", "content": reply})
    return {"reply": reply}
Rate-limit screenshot requests per session and add an allowlist of domains if the tool is user-accessible. Never fetch internal or private network URLs.

Browser MediaRecorder captures audio as WebM/Opus. The server transcribes it with Whisper and injects the transcript as a normal user text message. This keeps the Claude API calls unchanged — voice is a pre-processing step, not a different code path.

import io, tempfile
from openai import AsyncOpenAI          # Whisper via OpenAI SDK
from fastapi import UploadFile

openai_client = AsyncOpenAI()

async def transcribe_audio(audio_file: UploadFile) -> str:
    """Transcribe uploaded audio using Whisper."""
    raw = await audio_file.read()
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".webm", delete=False) as tmp:
        tmp.write(raw)
        tmp_path = tmp.name
    with open(tmp_path, "rb") as f:
        result = await openai_client.audio.transcriptions.create(
            model="whisper-1", file=f, response_format="text"
        )
    return result.strip()

@app.post("/chat/{session_id}/voice-message")
async def voice_message(session_id: str, audio: UploadFile):
    transcript = await transcribe_audio(audio)
    # Now treat the transcript as a regular text message
    history = conversations.setdefault(session_id, [])
    history.append({"role": "user", "content": transcript})
    response = await client.messages.create(
        model="claude-opus-4-8", max_tokens=2048, messages=history
    )
    reply = response.content[0].text
    history.append({"role": "assistant", "content": reply})
    return {"transcript": transcript, "reply": reply}
Rendering Attachments in the Chat Transcript
When a user sends an image alongside text, store a preview URL and render it above the text bubble. Clicking the thumbnail expands it to full size.

When the agent calls an image-generation tool (e.g. a DALL-E or Stable Diffusion tool), the tool result returns a URL or base64 image. Render it inline in the assistant message, similar to an attachment preview.
Any user-uploaded media requires safety checks before it reaches the model:
: verify the declared content-type matches the file magic bytes; reject mismatches.
: enforce before reading the file into memory to prevent memory exhaustion.
: for consumer-facing products, run uploaded images through a content moderation API (e.g. AWS Rekognition SafeSearch) before forwarding to Claude.
: for screenshot features, restrict to public HTTP/S URLs; block private ranges (192.168.x.x, 10.x.x.x, localhost) to prevent SSRF attacks.
: limit page count before rendering to avoid out-of-memory crashes from render bombs (PDFs with thousands of invisible objects).

import pytest
from httpx import AsyncClient, ASGITransport
from app import app
import io

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_image_upload_rejects_large_file():
    big_image = b"\x89PNG\r\n" + b"0" * (6 * 1024 * 1024)
    async with AsyncClient(transport=ASGITransport(app=app), base_url="http://test") as ac:
        resp = await ac.post(
            "/chat/sess1/message-with-image",
            data={"text": "analyse this"},
            files={"image": ("big.png", io.BytesIO(big_image), "image/png")},
        )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    assert "exceeds" in resp.json()["error"]

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_pdf_to_image_blocks_fallback(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.pdf_to_image_blocks",
                        lambda b, max_pages=10: [{"type": "text", "text": "extracted text"}])
    blocks = pdf_to_image_blocks(b"fake-pdf", max_pages=5)
    assert blocks[0]["type"] == "text"
    assert "extracted" in blocks[0]["text"]

Multimodal UX adds image upload, PDF analysis, URL screenshots, and voice input to the standard chat interface. All non-text inputs are converted to base64 image blocks or plain text before reaching Claude, keeping the API layer uniform. The main engineering concerns are file validation, size limits, content safety scanning, and SSRF prevention for URL inputs. On the output side, model-generated images flow back as tool results and render inline in the assistant message alongside streamed text.
